title: cipadessa baccifera var. rothii
intro: Cipadessa baccifera var. rothii, also known as the Indian albizia or siris, is a plant species that is native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional remedies such as ointments and poultices.
